title: Interleaving String
description: Given strings s1, s2, and s3, find whether s3 is formed by an interleaving of s1 and s2. An interleaving of two strings s and t is a configuration where s and t are divided into n and m substrings respectively, and s3 is formed by merging s1 and s2 in a way that maintains the left-to-right order of characters in each string.
constraints: s1, s2, and s3 consist of lowercase English letters
thinking_questions: dp[i][j] = can s1[:i] and s2[:j] interleave to form s3[:i+j]. What are the transitions?
What does it mean to 'take from s1' vs 'take from s2'?
What is the base case dp[0][0]?

hints.L1: 2D DP: dp[i][j] = can s1[:i] and s2[:j] interleave to form s3[:i+j].
hints.L2: dp[i][j] = (dp[i-1][j] and s1[i-1]==s3[i+j-1]) OR (dp[i][j-1] and s2[j-1]==s3[i+j-1]). Use 1D rolling array.
hints.L3:
# Interleaving String — O(m*n) time, O(n) space
# KEY INSIGHT: dp[i][j] = can we form s3[:i+j] using s1[:i] and s2[:j]

def isInterleave(s1, s2, s3):
    m, n = len(s1), len(s2)
    if m + n != len(s3):
        return False

    dp = [False] * (n + 1)
    dp[0] = True  # empty strings interleave to form empty string

    # Fill first row (using only s2)
    for j in range(1, n + 1):
        dp[j] = dp[j-1] and s2[j-1] == s3[j-1]

    for i in range(1, m + 1):
        dp[0] = dp[0] and s1[i-1] == s3[i-1]  # first col (only s1)
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            k = i + j - 1  # index in s3
            dp[j] = (dp[j] and s1[i-1] == s3[k]) or                     (dp[j-1] and s2[j-1] == s3[k])

    return dp[n]

follow_up_questions: Why is length check (m+n != len(s3)) an important early exit?
How do you debug the dp transitions mentally?
Could you solve this with BFS treating each state as (i, j)?
solution_notes: 2D DP compressed to 1D. dp[i][j] = can s1[:i] and s2[:j] form s3[:i+j]. Transitions: take from s1 or take from s2.
